title: helleborine latifolia f. brevibracteata
intro: Helleborine latifolia f. brevibracteata, a lesser-known variant of the orchid family (Epipactis), is a perennial flowering plant native to various regions, including Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and promoting wound healing due to its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties. Helleborine latifolia f. brevibracteata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
